data.name: ErrorBranchDiffRequiresCommits
data.value: Branch diff requires at least one commit in the repository. Create an initial commit first, then retry.

data.name: ErrorFileNotFoundAmongLocalChanges
data.value: File '{0}' not found among local changes (scope: {1})

data.name: ErrorFileNotFoundInLocalChanges
data.value: File not found in local changes: {0}

data.name: ErrorFileNotFoundInPullRequest
data.value: File not found in Pull Request: {0}

data.name: ErrorNoGitRepositoryRoot
data.value: No git repository root could be resolved. Set a repository path via --repo, MCP roots, or the localRepoPath configuration.

data.name: ErrorNotInsideGitRepository
data.value: Error: Not inside a Git repository. Run this command from a Git repository root.

data.name: ErrorPageNumberOutOfRange
data.value: Page number {0} is out of range. Valid range: 1 to {1}.

data.name: LogLocalGitClientNoHead
data.value: Repository at {Root} has no HEAD (no commits yet); using fallback commands

data.name: LogLocalReviewProviderFetchingFileDiff
data.value: Fetching local file diff: path='{Path}', scope={Scope}, root={Root}

data.name: LogLocalReviewProviderFileDiffCompleted
data.value: Local file diff completed: path='{Path}', scope={Scope}, {HunkCount} hunk(s), {ElapsedMs}ms

data.name: LogLocalReviewProviderFileNotFound
data.value: File '{Path}' not found in local changes (scope={Scope})

data.name: ErrorLocalContradictionGuard
data.value: git status reports {0} modified file(s) for scope '{1}', but the unified diff returned no content. This is a transient git failure (likely concurrent index access from your IDE's git extension or AV process-startup contention). Re-run the review; if it persists, check the server log for git stderr messages.

data.name: ErrorPrContradictionGuard
data.value: PR #{0} metadata reports {1} changed file(s), but the diff returned no content. This indicates an upstream provider failure (e.g. transient API error). Re-run the review; if it persists, check the server log for provider errors.

data.name: LogMcpWorkspaceRootProviderUnexpandedVariable
data.value: CLI --repo value '{Path}' looks like an unexpanded variable (e.g. from VS Code on a non-VS Code client). Ignoring it — MCP roots or localRepoPath will be used instead.

data.name: LogPageAllocatorEmptyCandidates
data.value: Empty candidates, returning single empty page

data.name: MsgMcpServerRepoHint
data.value: The MCP server will be launched with --repo pointing to your workspace.

data.name: MsgRestartIdeHint
data.value: Restart your IDE or reload the MCP client to pick up the new configuration.

data.name: WarnEmbeddedPromptResourceNotFound
data.value: Warning: Embedded prompt resource not found: {0}

data.name: MsgChooseAgentPrompt
data.value: Which AI agent will you use with REBUSS.Pure? [1] GitHub Copilot (default) [2] Claude Code

data.name: MsgBackedUpMcpConfiguration
data.value: Backed up existing MCP configuration to: {0}

data.name: ErrMcpConfigLocked
data.value: Could not write MCP configuration ({0}) at {1}: the file is locked, likely because the MCP client (e.g. Claude Code) is currently running. Close the client and rerun 'rebuss-pure init'. Underlying error: {2}

data.name: ClaudeSetup_ExplainBenefit
data.value: Claude Code CLI is not installed. REBUSS.Pure needs it to invoke Claude for review and self-review workflows.

data.name: ClaudeSetup_InstallingViaWinget
data.value: Installing Claude Code via winget (native standalone binary, no Node.js required)...

data.name: ClaudeSetup_InstallingViaBrew
data.value: Installing Claude Code via Homebrew (native standalone binary, no Node.js required)...

data.name: ClaudeSetup_InstallingViaNativeScript
data.value: Installing Claude Code via the official Anthropic install script (native standalone binary, no Node.js required)...

data.name: ClaudeSetup_NoNpmManualHint
data.value: Automatic install failed and 'npm' is not available for a fallback install. Install manually from https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview and rerun 'rebuss-pure init'.

data.name: ClaudeSetup_PromptNpmFallback
data.value: Native install failed. Fall back to 'npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code' (requires Node.js, which you already have)? [y/N]:

data.name: ClaudeSetup_AuthInstructions
data.value: Claude Code is installed but not authenticated. The next step launches 'claude' interactively so you can complete the built-in /login flow (opens a browser for your subscription or API key).

data.name: ClaudeSetup_PromptLaunchAuth
data.value: Launch Claude Code now to authenticate? [y/N]:

data.name: ClaudeSetup_VerifiedOk
data.value: Claude Code CLI verified and authenticated.

data.name: ClaudeSetup_DeclineBannerBody
data.value: Review and self-review workflows will not be available until Claude Code CLI is installed and authenticated. Install manually from https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview and rerun 'rebuss-pure init'.

data.name: CopilotSetup_ExplainBenefit
data.value: REBUSS.Pure can cooperate with GitHub Copilot CLI to provide a review flow that is resilient to IDE conversation summarization. This is optional — existing review tools work without it.

data.name: CopilotSetup_PromptInstallGhAndContinue
data.value: Install GitHub CLI to enable Copilot-powered review? [y/N]:

data.name: CopilotSetup_DeclineBannerBody
data.value: Existing REBUSS.Pure review tools are unaffected and will continue to work. The optional GitHub Copilot-powered review flow (resilient to IDE conversation summarization) will not be available until GitHub CLI is installed and authenticated with the Copilot scope.

data.name: CopilotSetup_ManualInstallHint
data.value:
To enable it later, install GitHub CLI (https://cli.github.com) and run:
gh auth login --web -s copilot
Or set REBUSS_COPILOT_TOKEN to a Copilot-entitled GitHub token.

data.name: LogCopilotClientStartFailed
data.value: Copilot SDK client failed to start: {Reason}

data.name: LogCopilotReviewPageFailed
data.value: PR {PrNumber} page {PageNumber} Copilot review attempt {Attempt} failed: {Reason}

data.name: LogCopilotReviewCompleted
data.value: PR {PrNumber} Copilot review completed: {Succeeded}/{Total} pages succeeded

data.name: ErrorAgentReviewLayerRequired
data.value: The AI review layer is required for code review. Set CopilotReview:Enabled to true in appsettings.json to enable it.

data.name: LogCopilotReviewUnavailable
data.value: Copilot review layer unavailable: {Reason}. Remediation: {Remediation}. TokenSource={TokenSource}, ConfiguredModel={ConfiguredModel}, EntitledModels=[{EntitledModels}], Login={Login}, Host={Host}

data.name: LogCopilotReviewAuthenticatedSuccess
data.value: Copilot authenticated as {Login} via {AuthType} (host={Host}). TokenSource={TokenSource}, Model={Model}

data.name: LogCopilotFreeTierShapeWarning
data.value: Copilot quota snapshot looks like a free tier — the configured model may silently downgrade mid-session. QuotaKeys=[{QuotaKeys}]

data.name: ErrorCopilotNotAuthenticatedRemediation
data.value: Copilot session is not authenticated. Run 'gh auth login --web' with Copilot scopes, then restart the MCP server. Alternatively set REBUSS_COPILOT_TOKEN (env var) or CopilotReview:GitHubToken (config) to a pre-minted Copilot-entitled OAuth token.

data.name: ErrorCopilotOverrideTokenRejectedRemediation
data.value: The override token supplied via {0} failed Copilot verification. Classic GitHub Personal Access Tokens are NOT valid Copilot-entitled tokens; you need an OAuth token with the 'copilot' scope minted by a first-party GitHub client. Run 'gh auth login --web' to obtain one, or remove the override and use the gh CLI session.

data.name: ErrorCopilotModelNotEntitledRemediation
data.value: Your Copilot session is not entitled to model '{0}'. Entitled models: [{1}]. Switch CopilotReview:Model in appsettings.json to one of the entitled models, or upgrade your Copilot subscription at https://github.com/settings/copilot.

data.name: ErrorCopilotStartFailureRemediation
data.value: Copilot SDK client failed to start: {0}. The SDK spawns the standalone Copilot CLI (from the '@github/copilot' npm package, NOT the 'gh copilot' extension). When the bundled native binary is missing from the SDK package, point the SDK at a system-installed copilot(.exe) by setting CopilotReview:CopilotCliPath (appsettings.json) or REBUSS_COPILOT_CLI_PATH (env var) to its absolute path, then restart the MCP server.

data.name: ErrorCopilotVerificationTimeoutRemediation
data.value: Copilot verification exceeded its deadline. The SDK did not respond within the expected window. Retry by restarting the MCP server; if the problem persists, check 'gh copilot --version' and the output of 'gh auth status'.

data.name: BannerCopilotNotAuthenticatedBody
data.value:
Your GitHub CLI login does not currently grant access to the configured Copilot model '{0}'.
Reason: {1} ({2}) {3}
To fix:
OPTION 1 — Re-run 'gh auth login' with Copilot scopes:
gh auth login --web
OPTION 2 — Set a pre-minted Copilot-entitled token (CI/headless):
set REBUSS_COPILOT_TOKEN=<token> (Windows) export REBUSS_COPILOT_TOKEN=<token> (Linux/macOS)
NOTE: Classic GitHub Personal Access Tokens are NOT valid Copilot-entitled tokens. You need an OAuth token minted by a first-party GitHub client with the 'copilot' scope.
OPTION 3 — Check your Copilot subscription:
https://github.com/settings/copilot
